reasoning: The reference value of 1.500 implies only ~6 ACLED-qualifying fatalities have occurred in China over the entire preceding 360 days (average 0.5 per 30-day period). A 10x multiplier would require 15 or more fatalities in a 30-day window. ACLED predominantly tracks organized political violence, protests, or state security actions, and explicitly excludes unorganized criminal mass violence or lone-wolf attacks (which explains why the 35+ deaths at the Zhuhai car ramming do not radically spike the baseline). Reaching >15 political fatalities in a single month is extremely unlikely without a massive, publicly visible event such as a major uprising or severe spillover conflict from Myanmar. No such events have occurred recently, keeping short-term odds at the probability floor, with slight upticks for more distant horizons given standard uncertainty.
